canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, multi-image understanding is consistently defined as a latent ability of models to correctly interpret and reason over inputs containing multiple images and interleaved text. This capacity is framed as a target for enhancement in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), with one paper describing it as a commonly discussed direction for future development. To operationalize and assess this ability, researchers employ a range of evaluation benchmarks. The most frequently cited instruments for measuring multi-image understanding in this set of papers are Mantis-Eval, NLVR2, and BLINK. Other benchmarks are also used to evaluate this construct, including MMMU, MVBench, MathVerse-mv, and SciVerse-mv. Additionally, one paper introduces MUIRBENCH as a benchmark specifically “designed to rigorously assess and evaluate multi-image understanding by multimodal LLMs.” The available data shows a consistent approach to conceptualizing and measuring this construct, focusing on model performance in multi-image scenarios.
papers: MIA-DPO: Multi-Image Augmented Direct Preference Optimization For Large Vision-Language Models
MMAD: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Industrial Anomaly Detection
MMIU: Multimodal Multi-image Understanding for Evaluating Large Vision-Language Models
MuirBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Robust Multi-image Understanding

definitions.text: The latent ability to correctly interpret and reason over inputs containing multiple images and interleaved text.

relationships.paper: mPLUG-Owl3: Towards Long Image-Sequence Understanding in Multi-Modal Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: First, we select five multi-image benchmarks: MMMU (Yue et al., 2024), BLINK (Fu et al., 2024), Mantis (Jiang et al., 2024), NLVR2 (Suhr et al., 2018), and MVBench (Li et al., 2024c). (Section 4.1)

relationships.paper: MuirBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Robust Multi-image Understanding
relationships.evidence: we introduce MUIRBENCH (MULTI-IMAGE UNDERSTANDING BENCHMARK), a comprehensive benchmark designed to rigorously assess and evaluate multi-image understanding by multimodal LLMs. (Section 2)
